channel.title: +द्विभाष्य-निखिलः on Vishvas's English lit
channel.description: Recent content in +द्विभाष्य-निखिलः on Vishvas's English lit

item.title: 2025 12 31 India
item.description:
Source: TW

I have decided to settle in India.

Nikhil Surya Dwibhashyam, 21 Dec. 2025 ed. 31 Dec. 2025

Intro

Happy winter! I plan to spend most of it in India—only seventeen more days here in this snow.

I’ve been doing some deeper thinking about my life recently, and unexpectedly easily resolved some long-standing questions. For example, though quitting my software engineering job last month made me seriously consider graduate school in Indology again, I’ve now given up on any dreams of acadēmīa for good, for the surprisingly simple yet entirely inescapable reason that my years of labor in a doctorate-program could come to naught in a single moment if a superior merely look up my name, find the various (already existing and future) posts “canceling” my religious–political beliefs (even if I took down my own web-site), and demand my removal from studentship or professorship. There are other reasons, e.g. the lower income and my age, but they’re all secondary.

item.title: Privacy canary
item.description:
Source: TW

Privacy policy, copy-right, & canary

Nikhil Surya Dwibhashyam

Privacy policy

My web-site is to be accessed only by readers aged twelve years or older. I do not believe that younger children should be allowed on the Internet by their parents. Note also that several pages on my web-site deal with sexual themes, from a religious stand-point.

My web-site uses (first-party, HTTPOnly) cookies for its pass-word protection and to prævent access by mobile or hostile users, and runs a small JS script mainly to help prævent access by mobile users. No other cookies are created or read and no other tracking is employed. As the (sole) administrator of this web-site, I have access to the following information about you based on your HTTP request. I do not collect any other information about users.

item.title: Will
item.description:
Source: TW

Last will & testament

Nikhil Surya Dwibhashyam, 13 Nov. 2023 ed. 7 Jan. 2026

This document constitutes my last will and testament, superseding all physical or electronic documents whose date præcedes this document’s date of last significant emendation shown above. If I be married at the time of my death, my Wife is permitted to override any stipulation in this document as she sees fit; none else is permitted to override even a single stipulation in this document. A wife is here defined as a woman whose marriage to me has been established by a Vēdic wedding (vivāhá) in which a bull or a cow is slain (gav-ālambhá), seven steps are taken by the fire (sapta-padī́), and the right hand is seized (hasta-grāhá). Thus, a woman with whom I am merely in a long-term relationship, or even a woman to whom I am legally considered married by some government’s system of “common law” without having completed a proper Vēdic wedding, cannot constitute a wife for the purposes of this document. (Conversely, a woman who has been Vēdically but not legally wed to me does constitute a wife.)
